output_debit_classifier.reason: Mondelez purchased a meal for factory production staff using a corporate credit card. The transaction explicitly classifies this as manufacturing overhead. Under IFRS, manufacturing overhead is a product cost that must be capitalized to inventory (work-in-process or finished goods), not expensed immediately as a period cost. The $125 increases the inventory asset account. The corporate credit card payment creates a liability (credit card payable) of $125 that will be settled when the credit card bill is paid. No tax is mentioned in the transaction, so no tax lines are included.
